title: Podman 3.0.2-dev on RHEL 8.3:  Error: cannot get namespace path unless container is running
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When I try to start a container in pod, the container is created, but not started, because the infra container exits with status code 1.  

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman pod create --name testpod -p 5000:5000`

2. `podman run -dt --pod testpod quay.io/libpod/alpine_nginx`

**Describe the results you received:**

> Error: cannot get namespace path unless container 27e3986409571271c1e27bbe801f80382d8d9441513ef9bb42b3a4bf146240f1 is running: container is stopped

The output of `podman ps -a`:

```
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                         COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS                     PORTS   NAMES
27e398640957  registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/pause:latest                        46 seconds ago  Exited (1) 40 seconds ago          e661af06de3d-infra
33694b43a153  quay.io/libpod/alpine_nginx                   nginx -g daemon o...  40 seconds ago  Created                            hardcore_torvalds

```

and the logs of the exited container:

```bash
$ podman logs e661af06de3d-infra
standard_init_linux.go:219: exec user process caused: no such file or directory
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

A running container in the pod.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.2-dev
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.7
Built:        Wed Apr  7 10:47:02 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/ppc64le
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


```
podman-3.0.1-6.module+el8.4.0+10607+f4da7515.ppc64le
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Can you upgrade to 8.4.0 (just released) and retest? For best results, recreate the pod - we're using a newer pause image on 8.4.0.
I have just upgraded to 8.4 and the problem persists.

The problem can actually be narrowed down to:

```bash
$ podman run registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/pause
standard_init_linux.go:219: exec user process caused: no such file or directory
```
This seems to be a ppc64le specific issue.

It does work when using another infra-image for the pod:

```bash
podman pod create --name testpod -p 5000:5000 --infra-image k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2
```
or

```bash
podman pod create --name testpod -p 5000:5000 --infra-image registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/pause:8.3-33
```

So it looks like registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/pause is bad?
@jnovy PTAL
The PPC64LE bit here makes me suspicious that the bug is specific to that architecture

I'm actually seeing something almost identical on s390x.

```
[root@rhel-vm]# podman version
Version:      3.1.0-dev
API Version:  3.1.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.16.1
Built:        Thu Mar 18 01:47:55 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/s390x

[root@rhel-vm]# podman logs 5ddbc5c08420-infra
standard_init_linux.go:219: exec user process caused: no such file or directory
```

This is with the `registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/pause:latest` image
```
[root@rhel-vm]# podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                                           COMMAND    CREATED         STATUS                    PORTS                   NAMES
e45c4d0226c3  registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/pause:latest                               10 minutes ago  Exited (1) 8 minutes ago  0.0.0.0:9200->9200/tcp  5ddbc5c08420-infra
```

Let's bump this back on the list.

I recommend going through the official Red Hat customer channels to report issues on Red Hat products.  This will help Red Hat engineers prioritize the issue.
Concur - especially since, IMO, this is probably an issue with the pause issue being shipped, not Podman.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since this is a RHEL issue, it should be handled via bugzilla not an upstream issue.
